subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, OECD, Eurostat, WJP, RSF, TI) + Finnish official (Statistics Finland, Bank of Finland, Migri) + Finnish press (Yle).
Standout drags: a fiscal deterioration / weak growth, a locked-in demographic decline, and a real Russia-border tail; the world's happiest country with top-tier institutions otherwise.
Source-lean: moderate Anglophone on hybrid-threat and happiness framing; macro data from Finnish/multilateral primaries.

economic.notes: Eurozone monetary credibility and sound banks, against a genuine fiscal/growth deterioration.

fiscal_state.notes: A widening deficit (~4.5% GDP) and debt approaching ~95% after a 2025 technical recession; the IMF warns of eroding debt-sustainability confidence. (IMF Art IV; Bank of Finland)

monetary_independence.notes: Eurozone - the ECB is credible and independent; no national control. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Eurozone member; the euro is the #2 global reserve currency, a modest shared benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: HICP near the ECB target (~2.4%), with a contained early-2026 electricity spike. (ECB; Statistics Finland)

banking_stability.notes: A sound, well-capitalised Nordic system; the watch-item is wholesale-funding reliance and Nordic-Baltic interconnection. (IMF; Bank of Finland)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Forestry/tech/machinery exports; the lost Russian market (absorbed) post-2022; stable policy, no resource curse. (OECD)

institutional.notes: Among the world's strongest institutions - top-tier rule of law, statistics, civil service, and press freedom.

statistical_integrity.notes: Statistics Finland is independent and uncompromised. (WJP; EC)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~#6, top-tier, with a slight global-trend dip. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: The world's happiest, highest-trust society, against labor unrest, a tightening migration stance, and a locked-in demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: A stable Orpo center-right coalition (thin majority), with recurring multi-sector strikes over labor/strike-law reform. (Yle)

civil_liberties.notes: Consistently among the freest, with strong protections. (WJP; RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A functional skilled path (EU Blue Card, specialist permits), but real 2024-26 tightening (higher pay floors, longer PR, a language test); work permits down. (Migri; TEM)

social_cohesion.notes: #1 World Happiness for years running with very high trust, but an immigration debate and youth-wellbeing decline pull the long horizon down. (World Happiness Report 2026)

demographics.notes: TFR ~1.26-1.3 (record-low band); the only Nordic with natural decrease - a locked-in long-term negative. (Statistics Finland)

geopolitical.notes: A strong new NATO member with a real Russia-border/hybrid-threat tail.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO member since 2023, with Forward Land Forces presence forming. (NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: A ~1,340 km Russia border closed indefinitely, with instrumentalized-migration and GPS-jamming hybrid threats - a real elevated tail. (Finnish Interior Ministry; Yle)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU regime, open capital account, no controls. (EC)

physical_practical.notes: Low climate exposure, excellent infrastructure, and very low crime; healthcare strong with access waits.

climate.notes: Among the clearest net beneficiaries near/mid-term - far-northern, top readiness, agricultural/forestry and winter upside - but the gain is windowed and erodes past warming-optimum overshoot. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6; Burke et al.)

healthcare.notes: Universal and strong, but with access waits, a 2023 county-reform strain, and 2025 copay hikes. (THL; OECD)

crime_safety.notes: Very low - a strength; a minor 2025 uptick off a low base. (Police/Statistics Finland)

summary: Among the world's strongest institutions, the highest-trust and happiest society, low crime, low climate exposure, and eurozone monetary credibility. The drags are a genuine fiscal/growth deterioration, a locked-in demographic decline (the only Nordic in natural decrease), a tightening migration stance, and a real Russia-border/hybrid-threat geopolitical tail as a new NATO member.
